scope: Week9 canary approval for C/D level traffic only

canary_window.name: Week9 C/D only canary
canary_window.start_condition: Release owner confirms health checks, backup, and C-08 smoke evidence before enabling traffic.

notes: Only users currently classified as C or D may enter the Week9 canary.
S/A users remain manual_premium and must not be auto-cut over.
B users remain ai_assisted and are reserved for H-08 Week10-11 observation.

entry_gates: C-08 J-02 AI end-to-end smoke is passed.
J-01 level smoke proves C and D produce ai_auto routing.
/health/detail returns api, db, and redis as ok.
A backup newer than 24 hours exists before enabling canary.
Admin conversation list loads for operator review.
No active P0/P1 incident is open for Telegram ingress, AI reply, payment, or database health.

required_checks: messages by sender_type in the last 24 hours
success_indicators: Assistant replies continue after user messages.
WAITING_OPERATOR and HUMAN_LOCKED queues do not grow unexpectedly.
No C/D canary user is incorrectly routed to manual_premium.
No S/A/B user is included in the canary cohort.
Stripe orders may remain pending unless a test payment is intentionally completed.

pause_conditions: Assistant replies fail for more than 10 minutes.
Two users fail onboarding during the canary window.
Any S, A, or B level user enters the canary cohort.
Any data-loss or privacy exposure symptom appears.
D8 beta report cannot be generated when requested.

rollback_plan.primary_action: Disable the Week9 C/D canary feature flag or set eligible traffic percent to 0.
rollback_plan.secondary_action: If code rollback is required, preserve evidence and return production to the previous stable commit.
resume_conditions: Incident note or release note records the pause and fix.

product_acceptance: Week9 canary is approved for C/D levels only.
S/A/B levels are explicitly excluded from Week9 auto cutover.
Entry gates, monitoring cadence, pause conditions, and rollback plan are documented.
Approval is ready to unblock P5-09 feature flag implementation.

final_confirmation.confirmation_text: H-01, H-02, H-05, H-07, H-08, H-09, H-10, H-11 all confirmed approved.
